summary: The team is in full execution mode on two major initiatives: the 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' report and the Intelligent China program launch, with SEO prioritization shifting focus to Asia Star and Intelligent China over US partnerships.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint CKGSB-IE report 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', targeting a digital version by early September for Singapore events (Tech in Asia, Tyson). This week, the team must translate the bulk of the Chinese report, identify op-ed contributors, and explore WeChat alumni case studies; Felix is meeting with Tech in Asia and Tyson tomorrow. Simultaneously, the Intelligent China program webpage launches this week, with the SCM campaign being planned and the Asia Star campaign running two parallel routes (AI vs. traditional) for the first month. Urgent this week: finalize the Shun Tian Shu press release (Jimmy), provide SEO brief and search area list (Joseph), and follow up with social media agencies (Kayleigh) as Media Minds is prioritized. Upcoming: the IE report translation and op-ed curation will ramp up, and the team will analyze Asia Star participant profiles for CRM insights.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to improve search ranking for programs like Asia Star and Intelligent China, prioritizing these two programs over US partnerships for initial focus.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on website menu, progress on brochures and videos, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch events at Tech in Asia and Tyson conferences in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), discussed new 'Intelligent China' program launching in December, and planned SCM campaign for the new program.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

actionItems.detail: Need detailed description of each conversion event (e.g., download report, BCI time) from the SEO manager.

actionItems.detail: Include target focus and prompts for AI, EVs, smart manufacturing in China.

actionItems.detail: Identify profiles (company type, age, location) to inform targeting.

actionItems.detail: Julie to review menu and give feedback in writing or meeting this week.

actionItems.detail: Julie to send one ready article and work on rest this week.

actionItems.detail: Jimmy to revise press release to include 10 points or convert to 10-point article.

actionItems.detail: Kayleigh to respond to Carnegie and have call with Impactable; expedite process.

actionItems.detail: Kayleigh to wait for Li Wei's input before dividing translation work.

actionItems.detail: Outline report structure, title, promotion plan.

actionItems.detail: Translate the main research from Professor Leeway.

actionItems.detail: Reach out to President Santiago, Dean, and alumni like Tu Tian.

actionItems.detail: Confirm event themes and possibility of launching report at their conferences.

actionItems.detail: First month of Asia Start campaign will test AI vs traditional approaches.

actionItems.detail: Website is nearly ready and needs to be launched.

actionItems.text: Plan SCM campaign for Intelligent China program.
actionItems.detail: Once webpage is done, start SCM campaign; decide on AI or traditional route.

actionItems.detail: Professor Zhu Yang requested revisions to the article table and a take-home message.

actionItems.text: Start translation work on the IE University report once aligned.
actionItems.detail: Joseph agreed to start translation work on the report with IE University.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO and marketing efforts.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; Asia Star next edition in early November.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or converting to 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to budget constraints and time.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target to have digital version ready by early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will target foreigners, with modules in Beijing, Shanghai/Hangzhou, and Shenzhen; focus on module one first.
Use Temu as the second case study for the IE University report instead of halocoko.
Place key findings at the beginning of the IE University report.
Professor Li Wei confirmed support for the overall plan of the 2026 English report 'China’s New Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: 2026年英文报告:  China’s New Playbook for Going Global
